# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 72

This is turn 72. Turns 1 through 71 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 72.

## Turn 72 — Wall doubling makes the rank–Arf identity rigorous

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation remains implicit, and \(40491355905\) is the verifier residue of the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\). 

Turn 71 used

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)
=
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)\pmod 2,
\qquad
q_U(x)=q(x)+B(x,U^{-1}x),
\]

but left “Wall reduction” compressed.  Here is a direct proof.

### 1. Relative Arf–Dickson lemma

Let \((V,q)\) be nonsingular over \(\mathbf F_2\), with polar form \(B\), and let \(U\in O(V,q)\) have \(2\)-power order. Put

\[
N=1+U,\qquad R=NV,\qquad K=\ker N.
\]

First,

\[
B(x,U^{-1}x)=B(x,Ux)=q(Nx),
\]

so

\[
q_U(x)=q(x)+q(Nx).
\tag{72.1}
\]

Its polar form is

\[
B_U(x,y)=B\!\left(x,(1+U+U^{-1})y\right).
\]

Because a \(2\)-power-order operator over \(\mathbf F_2\) is unipotent, \(1+U+U^{-1}\) is \(1\) plus a nilpotent operator and is therefore invertible. Thus \(q_U\) is nonsingular.

Define the Wall form on \(R\) by

\[
\omega(Nx,u)=B(x,u),\qquad u\in R.
\tag{72.2}
\]

This is well-defined: changing \(x\) by an element of \(K\) does not change the value because

\[
R=K^\perp.
\]

It is nondegenerate, and

\[
\omega(v,v)=q(v).
\tag{72.3}
\]

Now consider the nonsingular quadratic space

\[
(V,q_U)\perp(V,q).
\]

The subspace

\[
M=\{(k,k):k\in K\}
\]

is totally singular. Moreover,

\[
M^\perp/M\cong R\oplus R
\]

through

\[
[(x,x+u)]\longmapsto (Nx,u).
\]

Under this identification, the induced quadratic form is

\[
\Phi_\omega(v,u)
=
\omega(v,v)+\omega(u,u)+\omega(v,u).
\tag{72.4}
\]

Consequently,

\[
[q_U]+[q]=[\Phi_\omega]
\]

in the quadratic Witt group of \(\mathbf F_2\).

### 2. Wall-doubling lemma

For every nondegenerate bilinear form \(\omega\) for which \(\Phi_\omega\) is nonsingular,

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(\Phi_\omega)
\equiv \dim R\pmod 2.
\tag{72.5}
\]

This follows by elementary elimination.

If \(v\mapsto\omega(v,v)\) is nonzero, choose \(e\) with \(\omega(e,e)=1\). Then

\[
(e,0),\ (0,e)
\]

span the anisotropic plane

\[
a+b+ab,
\]

of Arf invariant \(1\). Orthogonal projection away from this plane gives the same doubled construction for the bilinear Schur complement on a space of dimension \(\dim R-1\).

If \(\omega(v,v)=0\) for every \(v\), then \(\omega\) is alternating, so \(\dim R\) is even. Splitting one symplectic plane from \(R\) splits a hyperbolic four-space from \(\Phi_\omega\), changing neither the Arf invariant nor the dimension parity.

Induction gives (72.5). Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)
=
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
\pmod 2.
}
\tag{72.6}
\]

This proves the rank–Dickson identity used in Turn 71 without a transvection factorization or coupled-orbit Gauss-sum casework.

As a finite audit, I exhaustively checked the doubling lemma for every invertible bilinear matrix in dimensions \(1,2,3,4\): whenever \(\Phi_\omega\) was nonsingular, its Gauss sum was exactly

\[
(-2)^{\dim R},
\]

equivalent to (72.5).

### 3. Uniform tame-module application

Let \(V\) now be a ramified self-dual simple tame module, and put

\[
U=S^{\omega _2}.
\]

Let \(W\) be its irreducible inertia constituent and write

\[
V|_I\simeq W^{\oplus s},
\qquad
f=\dim_{\mathbf F_2}W=2^a r,
\quad r\ \text{odd}.
\]

Self-duality gives an involution on

\[
\operatorname{End}_I(W)\simeq\mathbf F_{2^f}.
\]

The restriction of \(q\) is the trace of a nonsingular Hermitian form on an \(s\)-dimensional multiplicity space. Each Hermitian line is a minus-type norm–trace quadratic space, hence

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q)\equiv s\pmod2.
\tag{72.7}
\]

The operator \(U\) permutes the \(f\) inertia weights in \(r\) cycles of length \(2^a\), each with multiplicity \(s\). The power \(U^{2^a}\) commutes with both tame inertia and Frobenius and is a central \(2\)-element. It therefore acts trivially on the simple characteristic-two module \(V\). Each cycle consequently contributes an \(s\)-dimensional fixed space, so

\[
\dim V^U=rs.
\]

Hence

\[
\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
=
fs-rs
=
rs(2^a-1)
\equiv s\pmod2.
\tag{72.8}
\]

Combining (72.6)–(72.8),

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(q_U)=s+s=0.
\]

Thus the candidate determinant obstruction is hyperbolic for every ramified self-dual simple tame module, including all scalar-twist branches.

In the unramified case \(U=1\), so \(q_U=q\), and the norm–trace calculation gives Arf invariant \(1\). This agrees with the local Kummer result: the deep-unit subspace makes the ramified local form hyperbolic, while the unramified local form is minus type. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the relative Arf–Dickson identity used in Turn 71 by an explicit Wall-form quotient and doubling argument. Combined it with the inertia-multiplicity calculation to prove uniformly that the actual \(A_2\) determinant form is hyperbolic on every ramified self-dual simple tame module and minus type on every unramified one.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The decorated marked chief-factor induction and local Kummer theorem still need consolidation into one fully formal proof note.

**NEXT**: Insert this Wall-doubling lemma as the candidate-side determinant proposition, then assemble it with nonsplit transgression, shearing, scalar decorations, and the marked induction.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
